title: #[non_exhaustive] on SecretError is correct and must be kept
description: SecretError (core lib:422) is marked #[non_exhaustive]. A contributor doing 'match err { NotFound => ..., Backend => ..., ... }' will get a compile error and ask why this annotation is needed. Reason to keep: new error variants will be added as backends evolve (e.g. a RateLimited variant). Without #[non_exhaustive] downstream code with exhaustive matches would break on a minor version bump. The annotation is correct and must be preserved even if it creates mild friction for downstream pattern-matching.
